g.text: These envelope fields are omitted here for clarity except
where transactionId is contextually relevant, e.g., the
periodic meter value events in the charge loop.
Provide Available PmaxSchedule or Tariff Table
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Started, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = CablePluggedIn)
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = Authorized)
[Default tariff configured for EVSE (independent station-level config)]
Discharge fields of V2XChargingParametersType are omitted
[Until EVSEProcessing = Finished (SECC awaits CSMS profile internally)]
to bind the profile to the active transaction.
Alternative: AbsolutePriceSchedule for exact currency prices.
EV displays tariff to driver before accepting session.

EVSE adjusts output voltage to match EV battery voltage
Per [V2G20-1546]: in Scheduled Control Mode the EVCC SHALL
send BOTH EVPowerProfile and ScheduleTupleID when ChargeProgress
is Start, Standby, Stop, or Pause. ScheduleTupleID is carried inside
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = Charging, triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged)
the EVCC SHALL provide either EVTargetVoltage
NEVER both in the same message.
the schema; in practice the unused field is sent

ISO 15118-20 renegotiation uses parallel side stream
Update Available PmaxSchedule or Tariff Table
Note: in K16 the profile is delivered BEFORE the side
is sent during the side stream sequence (Figure 139).
in ChargeLoopRes on the mainstream channel.

[K17: EV-initiated (CSMS contacted on side stream, per Figure 141)]

Discharge fields are omitted in unidirectional DC.
Per OCPP 2.1 [K17.FR.07]: CSMS SHALL send a
and at most three chargingSchedule elements. The transactionId
binds the profile to the active transaction.
Per OCPP 2.1 K16 Figure 139, the SECC does NOT send
NotifyEVChargingNeedsRequest and the CSMS does NOT send
a new SetChargingProfileRequest on the side stream;
the profile was already delivered in the K16 trigger block above.
ScheduleExchangeRes returns directly without an Ongoing loop.
Alternative: AbsolutePriceSchedule for exact currency prices.
EV displays tariff to driver before accepting session.

End Charging and Stop OCPP Transaction
Per [V2G20-1546]: in all control modes EVPowerProfile,
and in Scheduled Control Mode also ScheduleTupleID,
shall be sent when ChargeProgress is Start, Standby,
Stop, or Pause. ScheduleTupleID is carried inside
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated, chargingState = EVConnected, triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged)
